Records.description: Divergent one-loop triangle $I_3^D(m_2^2,s,m_3^2;0,m_2^2,m_3^2)$ with two massive and one massless internal line. Contains a soft singularity with $1/\varepsilon$ pole. Result obtained from Beenakker-Denner via replacement rule. Given in terms of $\operatorname{Li}_2$ and logarithms.

One-loop three-point function with two massive internal propagators (masses $m_a, m_b$) and one massless propagator, with all three external legs off-shell ($q_1^2, q_2^2, q^2 = (q_1+q_2)^2$ generic). The maximal cut in $d=4$ is computed by direct integration and shown to equal $1/\sqrt{(q^2+q_1^2-q_2^2)^2 - 4 q^2 q_1^2}$, independent of the internal masses (Eq. 3.10). The full d-dimensional homogeneous solution is given in Eq. 3.16. Used as a sub-loop building block for the two-loop families. The variant with one squared (dotted) massive propagator is shown to have vanishing maximal cut at $d=4$ (Eq. 3.20).
Records.familyName: Tri (one-loop triangle, two massive propagators, three off-shell legs) / one-loop triangle two-mass

Records.description:
One-loop three-point function $I_3^D(
u_1, 
u_2, 
u_3; Q_1^2, 0, 0, M_1^2, M_2^2, 0)$ with two massive internal lines (masses $M_1, M_2$), one massless internal line, one off-shell external leg of virtuality $Q_1^2$ and two on-shell massless external legs. Evaluated with unit propagator powers ($
u_i = 1$) in $D = 4 - 2\varepsilon$. Expressed as sum of a Kampe de Feriet $S_1$ function and a Horn $H_2$ function (region IIIb representation), epsilon-expanded using MultiHypExp to obtain series coefficients as multiple polylogarithms / classical $\operatorname{Li}_{1,1}$. Used to validate agreement between the IIIb and IIb analytic continuations of Anastasiou-Ellis-Gehrmann-Melrose.
Records.familyName: one-loop triangle with two massive propagators and one off-shell leg (I_3^D)
Records.method: NDIM, hypergeometric representation, series expansion via differential equations, canonical form
Records.location: Section 5 (Application to the Feynman integrals), Eqs. (5.1)-(5.8)

Names: Tri (one-loop triangle, two massive propagators, three off-shell legs)
one-loop triangle with two massive propagators and one off-shell leg (I_3^D)
